sections.paragraph:
fem. (more rarely m., 1Ki 19:11), pl. רוּתֹות, רֻחֹות Jer 49:36.

(1) spirit, breath-

(a) breath of the mouth, Hauch des Mundes, fully, רוּחַ פֶּה Psa 33:6 (here spoken of the creative word of God), רוּחַ שְׂפָתַיִם Isa 11:4. Hence used of any thing quickly perishing, like syn. הֶבֶל Job 7:7 Psa 78:39. Often used of the vital spirit (Athem), Job 17:1 19:17 Psa 135:17 more fully, רוּחַ חַיִּים Gen 6:17 7:15 , 22 . הֵשִׁיב רוּחַ to return the breath, to respire, Job 9:18 compare No. 2.

(b) breath of the nostrils, snuffing, snorting, Job 4:9 Psa 18:16 Hence anger (compare אַף from אָנַף to breathe), Jud. 8:3 Isa 25:4 30:28 Zec 6:8 Pro 16:32 29:11 .

(c) breath of air, air in motion, i.e. breeze, Job 41:8 שָׁאַף רוּחַ to snuff up the breeze, Jer 2:24 10:14 14:6 רוּחַ הַיֹּום the breeze of the day, i.e. the evening, when a cooler breeze blows, Gen 3:8 compare Son 2:17 4:6 (Arab. رَوَاحُ evening, راح to do at evening). It is more often the wind, Gen 8:1 Isa 7:2 41:16 a storm, Job 1:19 30:15 Isa 27:8 32:2 . The air was supposed to be put in motion by a divine breath (see Exo 15:8 Job 15:30), and therefore the wind is called רוּחַ אֱלֹהִים the blast of God, 1Ki 18:12 2Ki 2:16 Isa 40:7 59:19 Eze 3:14 11:24 (not Gen 1:2 see No. 4) [it is clear that all these passages alike speak of the Spirit of God himself, and not of any wind supposed to be moved by the breath of God]. Wings are poetically ascribed to the wind, Psa 18:11 104:3 Hos 4:19 compare Ovid, Met., i. 264.

Wind is also used-

(aa) of a quarter of heaven. Compare רוּחַ הַקָּדִים the eastern quarter, Eze 42:16, 17 Eze 42:17comp. 18 Eze 42:18, 19 Eze 42:19אַרְבַּע רוּחֹות the four quarters of heaven, 37:9 1Ch 9:24.

(bb) of any thing vain, Isa 26:18 41:29 Mic 2:11. דִּבְרֵי רוּחַ vain words, Job 16:3. דַּעַת רוּחַ vain knowledge, 15:2 רְעוּת רוּחַ, רַעֲיֹון vain desire (comp. Gr. ἀέρα τύπτειν, Il. xx. 446; εἰς ἀέρα λαλεῖν, 1Co 14:9), see רְעוּת , רַעֲיֹון .

(2) i.q. נֶפֶשׁ No. 2, ψυχή, anima, breath, life, the vital principle, which shews itself in the breathing of the mouth and nostrils (see No. 1, a, b, whether of men or of beasts, Ecc 3:21 8:8 12:7 . Hence there is said חַיֵּי רוּחִי the life of my spirit (my life), Isa 38:16 חָֽיְתָה רוּחִי Gen 45:27 and שָׁבָה רוּחִי my spirit, life, returns, Jud. 15:9 1Sa 30:12, i.e. I revive. אֵין רוּחַ בֹּו “there is no spirit in it,” prop. said of dead and inanimate things, Eze 37:8 Hab 2:19 and metaph. used of any one stupified with astonishment and admiration, 1Ki 10:5. Sometimes the human spirit is called also רוּחַ אֱלֹוהַּ Job 27:3, as being breathed into man by God, and returning to him, Gen 2:7 Ecc 12:7 Psa 104:29 comp. Num 16:22. Twice in prophetic visions, spirit is used of a certain divine and miraculous power by which things otherwise inanimate are moved, Eze 10:17 Zec 5:9. [These passages do not apply; the Spirit of God is spoken of in the one, and the wind in the other.]

(3) animus, i.q. נֶפֶשׁ No. 3, the rational mind or spirit-

(a) as the seat of the senses, affections, and emotions of various kinds. Pro 25:28, “a man who does not rule רוּחֹו his affections,” or spirit, Gen 41:8, “his mind was agitated,” hither and thither; Dan 2:1 to this is ascribed both patience (אֶרֶךְ רוּחַ Ecc 7:8) and impatience (קֹצֶר רוּחַ which see); and fear ( Isa 61:3); and strength of the mind ( Jos 2:11 5:1 comp. Hab 1:11 Isa 19:13); and pride (גְּבַהּ רוּחַ, גָּבַהּ), and a quiet lowly mind (see קַר and שָׁפָל ), affliction, Gen 26:25 Isa 65:14 Psa 34:19.

(b) as to the mode of thinking and acting, in which sense there is attributed to any one a stead-fast mind ( Psa 51:12); manly ( Pro 18:14); faithful ( Pro 11:13 Psa 32:2); and new and better, Eze 11:19 18:31 , etc. It is sometimes used of a spirit or disposition common to many, as רוּחַ זְנוּנִים (propensio ad scortationes, Geift der Hureren), Hos 4:12 רוּהַ עִוְעִים Isa 19:14 רוּחַ תַּרְדֵמָה 29:10 and such disposition (such a spirit) is said to be divinely given to men, and to be poured upon them from heaven, compare Eze 36:26, 27 Eze 36:27. Similar is Isa 28:6, “Jehovah shall be לרוּחַ מִשִׁפָּט ליֹּושֵׁב על חַמִּשְׁפָּט,” i.e. he will, as it were, fill all the judges with a spirit of justice.

(c) of will and counsel, hence חֵעִיר אֶת־רוּחַ פּ׳ to stir up any one’s spirit to any thing, 1Ch 5:26 2Ch 21:16 36:22 Ezr 1:1 Hag 1:14 Ezr 1:5 and in a sense not very different נָתַן רוּחַ בפ׳ to put an intention into any one, 2Ki 19:7 נָֽדְבָה רוּחַ פּ׳ whose mind, will, impels him, Exo 35:21 whence רוּחַ נְדְיבָה Psa 51:14 and then עָלָה עַל רוּחַ, i.q. עָלָה עַל לֵב to arise into the mind and to occupy the mind (as any counsel), Eze 20:32.- 1Ch 28:12, “a pattern of all things, אֲשֶׁר הָיָה בָרוּחַ עִמֹּו that he had in his mind” [surely The Spirit here must be the Spirit of God]. More rarely-

(d) it is applied to the intellect, Exo 28:3 Job 20:3 32:8 , 18 Job 32:18 Isa 29:24 40:13 Psa 139:7. [These two last passages, and, perhaps, more, belong to the Holy Ghost, and not to intellect.]

(4) רוּהַ הָאֱלֹהִים, רוּחַ יְהֹוָה the Spirit of God, rarely רוּחַ קֹדֶשׁ יְיָ the Holy Spirit of God (always with suff. רוּח קָדְשְׁךָ Psa 51:13 Isa 63:11, 12 Isa 63:12), more rarely κατʼ ἐξοχὴν הָרוּחַ Num 27:18 Hos 9:7 the divine power, which, like the wind and the breath, cannot be perceived, and by which animated beings live, Job 27:3 33:4 Psa 104:29 compare Gen 6:3 by which all the universe is animated, filled with life and governed (ζωοποιεῖται), Gen 1:2 Psa 33:6 Job 26:13 Isa 34:16 by which men are led to live both wisely ( Job 32:8) and honestly, Psa 51:13 143:10 [These various things said by Gesenius must be taken as a defective designation of the Holy Ghost himself.] Especially the Old Testament refers to the divine Spirit, peculiar endowments of mind, as that of an artificer, Exo 31:3 35:31 of a prophet, Num 24:2 1Sa 10:6, 10 1Sa 10:10 19:20 , 23 1Sa 19:23 Isa 42:1 59:21 (whence אִישׁ רוּחַ used of a prophet, Hos 9:7 and הָרוּחַ as a personification of the prophetic Spirit, 1Ki 22:21 2Ch 18:20); of an interpreter of dreams, Gen 41:38 also the courage of a military leader, Jud. 3:10 6:34 11:29 13:25 and kingly virtues, Isa 11:2, seqq.; and the same Spirit is given to some and taken away from others ( 1Sa 16:13, 14 1Sa 16:14), is transferred from one to another ( Num 11:17 2Ki 2:15); but in the golden age [the reign of the Messiah] it is to be conferred upon all men, according to Joe 3:1 Isa 44:3 59:21 . It is sometimes put in opposition to בָּשָׂר Isa 31:3 Zec 4:6 see בָּשָׂר No. 2 .

רוח

Chald. i.q. Hebr.-

(1) wind; pl. const. Dan 7:2.

(2) mind, Dan 5:20.

(3) the Spirit (of God), Dan 4:5 5:12 .
